title: Creamy Norwegian Russet Potato Stir-fry
description: A fresh and creamy dish featuring baked russet potatoes as a hangover cure, low-carb friendly with a touch of garlic and cream.
instructions: Slice the russet potatoes into rounds about 1/8-inch thick.
Sprinkle salt and pepper over both sides of the potato slices, then toss lightly with a fork or your hands until coated evenly.
Arrange the seasoned potato slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Brush each slice lightly with olive oil.
Bake in preheated oven for 25 minutes or until golden brown and crispy.
In a separate skillet, heat a bit of olive oil over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the heavy cream, reduce heat to low-medium, and stir occasionally until mixture thickens into a creamy sauce (about 5 minutes).
Remove potatoes from oven and let cool slightly. Gently fold cooked cream sauce over potato slices. Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.
Serve warm as a quick snack or light meal.
